reference: What brings you in? My child has been having a sore throat and chills yesterday as well. OK, so you mentioned the sore throat started three to four days ago and the chills started yesterday. And is he having any issues swallowing food or fluids? Yeah, it seems like appetite's gone down, like a lot of pain in his throat, especially when swallowing solid foods. Have you taken a look into his throat? Is it red? Do you see any yellow streaking white stuff, anything like that? I definitely did take a look yesterday and definitely saw a lot of redness. I don't know if I saw any white or yellow streaks, but definitely looks very red in there. OK, do his tonsils look enlarged? Uh, everything looks swollen in there, I don't know what's what, but it does look very angry in there. OK, OK. And has he had a stuffy nose or runny nose? No, not really, no. Any loss of taste or smell? No, nothing like that. OK, how about changes in vision or hearing? Any changes in her nails or skin like rashes? No, no rashes. OK. Have you taken his temperature at home? No, I think he might have had a fever yesterday, but we didn't really take a temperature. OK. And, any fatigue or exhaustion? Energy's pretty much the same, just really complaining a lot about the throat. OK, OK, have you noticed any swelling in in the neck area? Uhm, I think there might be like a few lumps near his neck, so I don't know if that's swelling, but definitely there is. There's a few that I felt over the last few days. OK, coughing? No, hasn't been coughing at all OK, um, and how about shortness of breath or wheezing? No, nothing like that. Chest pain? No. Feeling like his heart is racing? No. Swelling in his limbs? No. I haven't noticed anything like that. OK, dizziness or fainting spells? No. Headaches? He was complaining of a headache a few days ago, but it kind of went away on its own. OK. So the headache's gone completely now. Yeah, yeah. OK. Have any confusion or memory loss? No, not that I know. Joint pain or muscle aches? No. OK, how about his bowel movements? Have those been normal? P Yeah, they've been normal. No diarrhea or constipation. OK, how about in nausea or vomiting? No, no, he hasn't told me anything about that. Any appetite changes? Yeah, just with the trouble swallowing I'm trying to make sure that he gets like liquids because over the last few days he can't tolerate like solid foods at all. Yeah, how about a weight loss over the past few weeks? No, not really. t's just been over the last few days that he hasn't been eating like himself. OK. Alright and has he been healthy otherwise? Yeah, otherwise he's a very healthy child. We have rarely gone to a doctor for him before, so yeah, nothing. Nothing else with him. OK, have you tried anything for his sore throat? Anything over the counter? We did try, uh, like Tylenol cold. It helped with this headache, but it didn't really help with his sore throat OK, and has he ever experienced anything like this in the past? No, not that I know of. OK. Any hospitalizations or surgeries in the past? No. Any medications that he's on? No, no medication. OK, is he up to date with all his immunizations? Yeah, all his immunizations are up to date. OK, any allergies? No, not that I know. OK, family history of lung conditions or heart conditions? No, no lung or heart conditions. His father does have diabetes though. OK, how old was he when he was diagnosed with diabetes? I think probably in his late 30s with type 2 diabetes. Type 2, OK and then any sick contacts recently that he may have had? I don't think at home at least, uh, but maybe at school, like you never know who they are in contact with, so I'm wondering if he just picked it up somewhere from school. OK. Yeah, yeah, they're all wearing masks right now, but I don't know if he picked something something up from his classmates. Yeah for sure. OK, and then any travel to anywhere recently? No, we've been at home, haven't left even to go to like a different city, have been in London the whole time. OK, yeah, and who does he live with at home? So he lives with myself and his dad. Any siblings? No, he's an only child. OK, any reason to believe you have old carpets, mold, asbestos, anything that could be triggering his symptoms? You know we don't have any pets. We don't have any like old carpets, it's a fairly new apartment, so I don't think any of that should be an issue. Okay, well, I think those are all the questions that I had for him. Anything that you wanted to add or anything that I missed today? Uh, no, I think those are everything I wanted to talk about but I just was wondering if he needs to get tested or like if there's anything we can do today for him. Yeah, so he does seem to be checking off some of the criteria for strep throat. So just to make sure, because he does have a score of right now at least three or four, or maybe even five, definitely over four, we can prescribe him some antibiotics. But we will do a rapid strep test first in the clinic here today just to rule out in or rule that out. If it comes back positive and I guess even if it isn't positive because he checked off a lot of the boxes for strep throat, we will give him a prescription for penicillin or amoxicillin for 10 days. And then you can take that if his symptoms don't go away in a day or two. Because his symptoms also overlap with COVID, we will. do a swab for that as well and you should hear back within 24 to 48 hours and then you should isolate for 14 days. Does that sound good? Yeah, that sounds good. OK, great thank you. Thank you.
